It is prescribed to prevent and treat thromboembolic events such as deep vein thrombosis, pulmonary embolism, stroke in atrial fibrillation, and systemic embolism with mechanical heart valves.\",cohort_count,gemini-2.5-flash","user_of_warfarin,Frequency,\"Warfarin use is frequently seen, especially in older populations, and cases can be readily identified in primary care, hospital, and insurance claim real-world health care data.\",cohort_count,gemini-2.5-flash","user_of_warfarin,Median age of incident cases,\"65 to 79 (Individuals typically present in their later adult years, often due to age-related cardiovascular conditions requiring anticoagulation.)\",cohort_characteristics,gemini-2.5-flash","user_of_warfarin,Percentage male,\"45% to 55% (The proportion of males and females using warfarin is relatively balanced, though it may vary slightly depending on the underlying indication.)\",cohort_characteristics,gemini-2.5-flash","user_of_warfarin,Survival at five years,50% to 80% (The 5-year survival among warfarin users varies widely depending on the severity of the underlying conditions for which it is prescribed.),cohort_survival,gemini-2.5-flash","user_of_warfarin,Cohort overlap,\"You would expect to see an overlap of 70-90% with hypertension, 30-50% with type 2 diabetes, 50-70% with hospitalised inpatient over a 5-year period, 90-100% with user of acetaminophen, 5-15% with user of morphine, and 20-40% for males with measurement of prostate specific antigen level. Overlap is expected due to shared risk factors for cardiovascular disease, the need for hospital care, and common medication use for pain management or other conditions.\",compare_cohorts,gemini-2.5-flash","user_of_warfarin,Cohort timing,\"Hypertension and type 2 diabetes typically precede warfarin initiation by many years. Hospitalised inpatient status can precede, be concurrent with, or follow warfarin initiation. Use of acetaminophen often precedes warfarin use by many years and is concurrent. Use of morphine is usually concurrent or follows, often in acute settings. Measurement of prostate specific antigen level usually precedes or is concurrent with warfarin use in older males, related to routine screening. Many chronic conditions like hypertension and type 2 diabetes typically manifest years before warfarin initiation, while hospitalisations and acute medication use can be concurrent or follow.\",compare_cohorts,gemini-2.5-flash","user_of_warfarin,Frequently seen comorbidities,\"Atrial fibrillation, Hypertension, Type 2 diabetes mellitus, Congestive heart failure, Ischaemic heart disease, History of stroke, History of deep vein thrombosis, History of pulmonary embolism, Peripheral arterial disease, Chronic kidney disease (These comorbidities often represent the primary indications for anticoagulation therapy or are common chronic conditions in the patient population requiring warfarin.)\",\"large_scale_characteristics, compare_large_scale_characteristics\",gemini-2.5-flash","user_of_warfarin,Frequently seen signs and symptoms,\"Irregular heartbeat, Palpitations, Shortness of breath, Chest pain, Swelling of leg, Redness of leg, Leg pain, Haematuria, Melena, Ecchymosis (These signs and symptoms are either indicative of the thromboembolic conditions warfarin is prescribed to treat or are potential adverse effects of anticoagulation.)\",\"large_scale_characteristics, compare_large_scale_characteristics\",gemini-2.5-flash","user_of_warfarin,Frequently seen medications,\"Aspirin, Metformin hydrochloride, Atenolol, Ramipril, Simvastatin, Furosemide, Digoxin, Paracetamol, Omeprazole, Levothyroxine sodium (These medications are frequently co-prescribed for managing cardiovascular diseases, diabetes, and other conditions common in patients requiring warfarin.)\",\"large_scale_characteristics, compare_large_scale_characteristics\",gemini-2.5-flash","user_of_acetaminophen,Clinical description,\"Acetaminophen is a widely used over-the-counter analgesic and antipyretic, primarily acting by inhibiting prostaglandin synthesis in the central nervous system to reduce pain and fever. It is typically used for symptomatic relief of mild to moderate pain and fever from various acute and chronic conditions.\",cohort_count,gemini-2.5-flash","user_of_acetaminophen,Frequency,\"Acetaminophen is one of the most frequently used medications globally, and cohorts of users can be readily identified in pharmacy dispensing data, primary care prescribing records, and hospital administration records.\",cohort_count,gemini-2.5-flash","user_of_acetaminophen,Median age of incident cases,\"30 to 50 (Individuals across all age groups, from infants to the elderly, use acetaminophen, with usage patterns varying depending on the indication.)\",cohort_characteristics,gemini-2.5-flash","user_of_acetaminophen,Percentage male,\"45% to 55% (The proportion of male users of acetaminophen is generally balanced with females, although specific pain conditions may show slight sex-based differences.)\",cohort_characteristics,gemini-2.5-flash","user_of_acetaminophen,Survival at five years,\"90% to 98% (As acetaminophen is typically used for symptomatic relief rather than for life-threatening conditions, the five-year survival of a general user cohort is expected to be high, reflecting general population mortality.)\",cohort_survival,gemini-2.5-flash","user_of_acetaminophen,Cohort overlap,\"You would expect to see an overlap of 30-60% with hypertension, 20-40% with type 2 diabetes, 20-50% with hospitalised inpatient, 5-15% with user of warfarin, 5-15% with user of morphine, and 10-20% with measurement of prostate specific antigen level. Acetaminophen use would typically follow diagnosis of type 2 diabetes by months to years. Entry into hospitalisation would typically coincide with or precede the initial use of acetaminophen for the acute event by hours to days. Acetaminophen use would typically follow initiation of warfarin by days to years. Acetaminophen use might precede, coincide with, or follow morphine use, often used as an adjunct, with timing varying from days to months. Acetaminophen use is typically independent of and not directly related in time to a prostate specific antigen measurement, preceding or following by an arbitrary duration of days to years. The timing often reflects acetaminophen being used to manage symptoms of pre-existing chronic conditions, or for acute issues that lead to hospitalisation or are managed alongside other medications.\",compare_cohorts,gemini-2.5-flash","user_of_acetaminophen,Frequently seen comorbidities,\"Headache, Osteoarthritis, Low back pain, Influenza, Common cold, Hypertension, Type 2 diabetes, Depression, Anxiety, Rheumatoid arthritis (These conditions either commonly cause pain or fever for which acetaminophen is used, or they represent highly prevalent chronic diseases in the adult population.)\",\"large_scale_characteristics, compare_large_scale_characteristics\",gemini-2.5-flash","user_of_acetaminophen,Frequently seen signs and symptoms,\"Pain, Fever, Myalgia, Headache, Arthralgia, Malaise, Sore throat, Cough, Nausea, Vomiting (These are the primary symptoms for which acetaminophen provides relief, reflecting its analgesic and antipyretic properties.)\",\"large_scale_characteristics, compare_large_scale_characteristics\",gemini-2.5-flash","user_of_acetaminophen,Frequently seen medications,\"Ibuprofen, Naproxen, Codeine, Aspirin, Diphenhydramine, Pseudoephedrine, Omeprazole, Amlodipine, Metformin, Atorvastatin (Users of acetaminophen may also take other over-the-counter pain relievers or cold/flu medications, or medications for common chronic conditions.)\",\"large_scale_characteristics, compare_large_scale_characteristics\",gemini-2.5-flash","user_of_morphine,Clinical description,\"Morphine is a potent opioid analgesic used to treat severe pain, often associated with acute conditions like surgery or trauma, chronic conditions such as cancer, or for palliative care. Cases can be readily identified in laboratory results from primary care, hospital records, and insurance claims data.\",cohort_count,gemini-2.5-flash","measurement_of_prostate_specific_antigen_level,Median age of incident cases,\"60 to 70 (Individuals typically present for prostate-specific antigen level measurement in their later middle age to older adult years, aligning with general screening recommendations and the increased incidence of prostate conditions with age.)\",cohort_characteristics,gemini-2.5-flash","measurement_of_prostate_specific_antigen_level,Percentage male,\"99% to 100% (Prostate-specific antigen is produced by the prostate gland, which is a male anatomical structure, thus virtually all individuals undergoing this measurement will be male.)\",cohort_characteristics,gemini-2.5-flash","measurement_of_prostate_specific_antigen_level,Survival at five years,\"85% to 95% (The overall five-year survival for a cohort undergoing prostate-specific antigen measurement is expected to be high, as many tests are for screening, monitoring benign conditions, or detecting highly treatable early-stage prostate cancers.)\",cohort_survival,gemini-2.5-flash","measurement_of_prostate_specific_antigen_level,Cohort overlap,\"You would expect to see an overlap of 40-70% with hypertension, 20-40% with type 2 diabetes, 10-30% with hospitalised inpatient, 5-15% with user of warfarin, 50-80% with user of acetaminophen, and 2-10% with user of morphine.\",compare_cohorts,gemini-2.5-flash","measurement_of_prostate_specific_antigen_level,Cohort timing,\"For people in the measurement of prostate-specific antigen level cohort who also appear in other cohorts, their entry into hypertension, type 2 diabetes, user of warfarin, and user of acetaminophen cohorts would typically be before or concurrent.
